blurb:
Jacquelyn has been living her best life in a foster home, playing with three other dogs and generally making herself the center of attention. She's about two years old, 58 pounds of Lab-Terrier mix, and her foster describes her as lovable, huggable, and sweet. She is absolutely a Velcro dog. If you sit down, she's next to you. If you walk into a room, she knows about it.

She responds to "sit," "no," and "back" and has been totally reliable overnight without any potty breaks. She does need a few let-outs during the day, so a work-from-home situation would be ideal. Worth knowing: Jacquelyn has a congenital kidney condition that's easily managed and hasn't slowed her down at all. Her foster is upfront that it may mean a shorter lifespan, but she's fully present and happy right now, and that counts for a lot.

She does great with friendly dogs and is excited to meet new ones. Cats are probably not the right fit, and toddlers would want to watch out for her enthusiasm. Older kids are worth a meet and greet. Mostly Mutts is a rescue organization based in Kennesaw. Head to their website to start an adoption inquiry and learn more about Jacquelyn.
scoring_notes:
• Velcro-dog personality paired with an honestly disclosed kidney condition creates a rare adopt-with-eyes-open narrative that builds trust with readers
• Congenital health detail adds emotional weight and positions this as a meaningful, intentional adoption rather than an impulse
• Household restrictions (no cats, no toddlers, work-from-home preferred) narrow the pool and suggest she may have been waiting longer than average

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
He was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten, had a good run with his family, and then circumstances changed. Now Iceman is five years old, settled in a foster home in Kennesaw, and ready for round two.

He is 11 pounds of easygoing. His foster profile is still filling in, but the picture that is coming together is a cat who likes hanging out with his people and watching the world go by. Not demanding, not chaotic, just a solid, companionable presence. He was in a foster home with four kids a few years back and loved every one of them, so a family with gentle kids should be just fine.

One thing worth knowing: he was not a fan of other male cats in his previous home. A female cat companion could work great, and if you already have one, it is worth a meet and greet. Dogs are an unknown, so that would need a slow intro. He is neutered, microchipped, up to date on vaccines, and combo tested negative. Adoption fee is $175.

Reach out to Mostly Mutts at adopt@mostlymutts.org or give them a call to ask about Iceman and set up a visit.
scoring_notes:
• Second-chance adult cat narrative resonates with readers who relate to life circumstances changing
• Easygoing, family-tested temperament with kids makes him broadly appealing to a wide adopter pool
• Foster profile still developing suggests a relatively recent return, limiting urgency signals

blurb:
Yasmeen is eight years old and has clearly figured some things out. She came to Mostly Mutts from animal control, went through a prison foster training program, and is now settled into a foster home where her mom says anyone who adopts her will hit the jackpot. That's a strong endorsement from someone who actually lives with her.

She took about a day to warm up and trust her foster, and once she did, she became a loyal, loving companion. She's good with cats (her foster has both male and female) and has met kids as young as seven without issue. She does have a protective streak when strangers come into her space, so the recommendation is to introduce new people outside the home first. It's a simple adjustment and she's been responding well to it. Dog-savvy teens and adults are probably the smoothest fit, and her trainer can be part of the meet and greet process if needed.

Yasmeen is not a complicated dog. She just needs to trust you first, and that apparently doesn't take long. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to their website to start an inquiry and meet Yasmeen.
scoring_notes:
• Animal control origin plus prison foster program plus foster's jackpot endorsement is a three-act redemption story that writes itself for a newsletter feature
• At eight years old she is the senior candidate most at risk of being overlooked, making her the highest-urgency adoption pitch in this group
• Protective-but-trainable trait is framed with a concrete solution, reassuring readers and broadening her appeal beyond just experienced-owner households

blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a little nubby tail, and energy to spare. She's a six-year-old Hound mix, about 35 pounds, and she came back to Mostly Mutts after a brief adoption that just wasn't the right fit activity-wise. That's not a knock on her. She's just a dog who wants a person (or family) that actually wants to get out and do things.

She's affectionate, playful, and genuinely fun to be around. Her foster puts her energy at a 7 or 8 out of 10, but says the cuddle level matches it. So you'd be signing up for adventures and then crashing on the couch together, which honestly sounds pretty good. She went through Mostly Mutts' prison training program and came out knowing a lot more than she went in with.

Josie does best as an only dog and needs a cat-free home. She's done some gentle mouthy play (never aggressive, just enthusiastic), so kids 10 and up are probably the better match. She hasn't met many kids yet, so a meet and greet is worth doing. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry and set up a time to meet Josie.
scoring_notes:
• Prison training program credential is a standout detail that signals transformation and gives readers a concrete reason to champion her
• Returned for activity mismatch is a relatable, non-alarming reason that reframes the bounce as a placement error, not a pet problem
• Only-dog, no-cat, older-kids-only restrictions combined with a return adoption suggest she has cycled through the system and needs the right match urgently

blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a little nubby tail, and enough energy to keep up with just about anyone. She's a six-year-old hound mix, about 35 pounds, and she came back to Mostly Mutts recently after a short adoption that wasn't the right fit. The family's energy level didn't match hers. So now she's looking for someone who actually wants an active dog.

She completed the Mostly Mutts prison training program, so she's got some real skills under her belt. Her foster puts her energy at a 7 or 8 out of 10, but says her cuddle level is just as high. She wants to go on adventures with you and then crash on the couch together. She's also mouthy in play, never aggressive, just enthusiastic, so older kids (10 and up) would probably be a better fit than little ones.

Josie does best as an only dog and without cats. If that's your situation and you've been waiting for a reason to get out more, she's a pretty compelling one. She's in a foster home now and ready to meet you.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Start with an inquiry on their website, then schedule a visit to come see her in person.
shelter_address: 2520 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Prison training program credential is a standout differentiator that signals investment and skill, building adopter confidence
• Returned due to energy mismatch reframes her as a dog waiting for the right active owner, not a problem pet
• Only-dog household requirement limits her pool, and a recent failed adoption adds quiet urgency to her story

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Gina came to Mostly Mutts as part of a Go-Gos themed litter, got adopted, and then life threw her a curveball. She developed some gastrointestinal issues, her adopter couldn't manage it, and back she came. Months of vet visits and food changes later, she's doing better. She still vomits sometimes and nobody has figured out exactly why. But here's the thing: her foster calls her the "best cat ever." That's a real quote from someone who feeds her the special food and cleans up after her and still says that.

She's about 11 months old and only 5 pounds, and she's probably going to stay pretty small. Independent enough to do her own thing, but she'll also find you on the couch when she wants to snuggle. She's playful, she likes the kids she's met, and she gets along fine with the two adult cats in her foster home.

The one practical note: because she eats a special diet, it's easiest if she's the only cat, or if you're willing to feed separately. Not a dealbreaker, just something worth thinking through before you go meet her. If you have a calm, cat-friendly dog at home, that could work too. She met a leashed dog and didn't even blink.

Gina is with Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue in Kennesaw. Reach out to adopt@mostlymutts.org or call to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Foster's 'best cat ever' quote cuts through skepticism about her medical history and makes her irresistible to the right reader
• Returned-adoption arc plus months of vet care signals meaningful shelter time and a story readers will root for
• Dietary caveat is clearly explained and manageable, keeping adoption barrier low despite the complexity

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
When you are getting your photo taken and another kitten is getting all the attention, the correct move is to stick your paw out until someone notices you. That is Ladybug's whole approach to life, and honestly it is hard to argue with.

She is about two months old, roughly two pounds, and came into Mostly Mutts from animal control with her siblings. She spent time in a foster home with adult cats and did great, then moved over to the Kitty Aquarium at the shelter while she waits for her person. She is litter trained, social, and already has a clear sense of her own personality. She likes snuggles, toys, and making sure she is included in whatever is happening.

She has started her kitten vaccines but will need those finished as she grows, and she will need to be spayed once she hits the right size. That is all handled through the adoption process, so nothing to worry about on your end. Dogs are unknown, but she has done well with other cats, so if you already have one at home, it is worth a meet.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Email adopt@mostlymutts.org or call to ask about Ladybug and get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Irresistible kitten personality with a standout visual hook makes her newsletter gold for driving immediate inquiries
• Social, litter-trained, and cat-compatible profile removes common adoption barriers for first-time cat owners
• Recently moved to shelter floor from foster suggests she is early in her wait and unlikely to be overlooked long

blurb:
Jacquelyn came in from animal control, landed in a foster home with three other dogs, and has basically been thriving ever since. She's two years old, about 58 pounds, and her foster describes her as lovable, huggable, and genuinely fun to be around. She's also a Velcro dog, so if you like having a shadow, she's your girl.

She knows sit, back, and no, and she's been great about going to the bathroom on a normal schedule, no late-night trips required. She does best with someone home during the day, or at least someone who can pop in midday. Her foster says she's a fantastic alert dog too. Nothing gets by her. Once you acknowledge it, she moves on.

One thing worth knowing: Jacquelyn has a congenital kidney defect. It's manageable and not affecting her day-to-day life at all, but it does mean her lifespan may be a little shorter than average. Her foster is upfront about it, and Jacquelyn clearly hasn't gotten the memo, because she is living fully. She loves other dogs, so bring your pup in for a meet and greet. She does better without cats, and kids are fine as long as they're not tiny toddlers she might accidentally bowl over with enthusiasm.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Submit an inquiry through their website to get started, then set up a time to come meet her.
shelter_address: 2520 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Congenital kidney condition adds rare emotional depth and urgency, making her the most compelling human-interest story in the group
• "Living fully" framing reframes her health challenge as inspiration rather than deterrent, ideal for newsletter storytelling
• Medical complexity and need for a home-present adopter narrows her pool, suggesting she has been and will continue waiting without the right spotlight

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Ladybug came into Mostly Mutts from animal control with her siblings, spent some time in a foster home, and recently moved into the shelter's Kitty Aquarium. She's about two months old and barely two pounds, so the whole thing fits in your hands right now.

She's the kitten who sticks her paw out when you're petting one of the others, just to make sure she's not forgotten. Not in an anxious way, just in a very "hey, I'm also here and I'm also great" way. She's social, litter trained, gets along with adult cats, and made such a strong impression during her photo shoot that whoever wrote her bio felt the need to mention it. That tracks.

She'll need to finish her kitten shot series as she grows, and her spay is required but will be completed once she's big enough. If you've been thinking about adding a kitten to your home, and especially if you already have a cat who might enjoy a small chaotic friend, she's worth a visit.

Ladybug is now at the Mostly Mutts shelter in Kennesaw. Stop by to meet her in the Kitty Aquarium, or reach out by email or phone to ask about next steps.
scoring_notes:
• Two-month-old kitten with paw-tap personality is a natural crowd-pleaser and the easiest adoption pitch in this batch
• Backstory is thin — came from animal control with siblings — offering little narrative depth for newsletter engagement
• Recently moved to the shelter Kitty Aquarium, signaling a very short wait that reduces urgency compared to other candidates

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Iceman was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten and spent five years with the same family. Then their situation changed, and through no fault of his own, he's back and looking for round two. He's five years old, 11 pounds, fully neutered, microchipped, vaccinated, and combo tested negative. He came in already knowing how to be a good houseguest.

His foster home is still getting to know him, but here's what's clear so far: he likes being around his people, he's litter trained without any fuss, and he's calm enough to just hang out and watch the world go by. He did great with four kids in a previous foster placement, so gentle kids at home should be just fine. He's also had good experiences with female cats, though he and male cats haven't always clicked. If you've got a female cat at home, there's a decent chance they'd get along.

He's a five-year-old cat who was already loved once and knows how to be part of a home. That's not a complicated sell. He's just ready to stop bouncing around and settle in somewhere.

Iceman is located in Kennesaw with Mostly Mutts. His adoption fee is $175. Email adopt@mostlymutts.org or call to set up a time to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Five years of domestic experience makes him one of the most turnkey adoption candidates in the group
• Five-year bond broken by owner circumstances is an emotionally resonant second-chance hook for newsletter readers
• Returned after a long prior adoption suggests he has been waiting again and deserves urgent spotlight attention

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
She came in as part of the Go-Gos litter, got adopted, and then life got complicated. Gina was returned after about a month when some stomach issues popped up, and she has been at Mostly Mutts ever since while the team figured out what was going on. They still do not have a clean answer, and she does vomit sometimes. But here is what they do have: a foster mom who calls her the best cat ever.

Gina is about 11 months old, around 5 pounds, and probably not going to get much bigger. She has held onto that kitten energy and kitten size, which honestly sounds like a pretty good deal. She is independent enough to do her own thing, but she loves to snuggle when she is ready. She gets along with the two adult cats in her foster home and was completely unbothered when she met a dog on a leash.

The one practical thing to know: she is on a special diet, so if you have other cats at home, you will want a plan for separate feeding. It is not a dealbreaker, just something to think through. If she would be your only cat, even better. She has been through a lot for a kitten, and she deserves someone who is in it for the long haul.

Mostly Mutts is based in Kennesaw. Reach out to adopt@mostlymutts.org or call to get the process started and ask about Gina.
scoring_notes:
• Returned kitten with unresolved health mystery creates emotional pull and reader empathy
• Foster mom's "best cat ever" endorsement is a powerful trust signal for hesitant adopters
• Special diet requirement and uncertain stomach issues suggest she's been harder to place and waiting longer than most kittens

blurb:
Yasmeen is eight years old, 58 pounds, and the kind of dog who takes a little while to trust you and then is completely devoted once she does. She came to Mostly Mutts from animal control, went through a prison training program, and is now settled into a foster home. Her foster mom says adopters will hit the jackpot with her, and based on what she's described, that tracks.

She's good with cats and has done well meeting other dogs, though she can come across as dominant at first before shifting into play mode. She loves her people deeply and does tend to guard them when strangers come around, so her new family should plan to introduce new visitors outside the home first. That's a small adjustment that makes a real difference for her, and she's been improving steadily.

For kids, she did great with a seven-year-old on a day trip and met some ten-year-olds at the shelter. Dog-savvy teens or adults are probably the smoothest fit, but her team is happy to do a meet with a trainer if you have kids and want to explore it.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Submit an inquiry through their website and plan to meet Yasmeen outside the shelter first so she can see you on neutral ground.
shelter_address: 2520 Cobb Place Blvd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144
scoring_notes:
• Eight-year-old senior from animal control with a full prison training investment is a high-urgency, high-reward adoption story
• Foster's "jackpot" endorsement paired with her loyalty and steady improvement arc gives readers a rooting interest
• Age, guarding behavior, and structured introduction requirements significantly narrow her adopter pool, pointing to a longer wait ahead without advocacy
